18.reason: [The function howhigh assumes that all node values are unique and uses value-based search to compute height, but BST may contain duplicates (as per problem: right child >= root). This can cause incorrect height calculation or infinite loops when duplicates exist. Also, the condition `p!=head` in howhigh is incorrect鈥攊t should not exclude the root from matching by value.]

33.reason: [This condition incorrectly excludes the root node (`head`) from being matched by value. If the leaf is the root (i.e., only one node), it will never match because `p == head`, so it returns 0. Also, using value equality is unsafe with duplicates.]
